$comment: Machine-readable Game Design Document for Secret Base Builder. This file is the source of truth for design intent. Human-readable companion: docs/GDD.md. Content data (actual module catalog, themes, environments) lives in src/content/*.json — this document defines the RULES those files must follow.

meta.title: Secret Base Builder
meta.audience: Kids roughly 5-12, plus anyone who ever drew a secret base on paper
meta.platform: Web (desktop mouse + tablet touch), no install, no accounts
meta.elevatorPitch: A 2D side-cutaway secret-base builder where kids drag rooms from a giant catalog into an underground grid and watch their dream hideout come to life in high-fidelity pixel art with a Spy Kids vibe: somewhat realistic, a bit techy, a bit fantastical.

designPillars.statement: The core joy is the sheer variety of modules and styles. Breadth of catalog > depth of mechanics.
implications: Adding a new module or style variant must require zero engine code changes — content is data.
The sidebar catalog must stay browsable at 100+ entries (scroll, filter, sort).

designPillars.statement: Pure creative sandbox. No resources, no costs, no wrong answers in v1.
implications: Placement rules exist only to keep bases coherent (no overlaps, stay underground), never to gate creativity.
Everything is instantly reversible: move, delete, undo.

designPillars.statement: Placed modules should feel like a living base, not stickers on a grid.
implications: Adjacent modules visually connect (doorways/openings at shared edges).
Architecture reserves hooks for future animated modules and wandering characters.

designPillars.statement: All art is placeholder until final styles are chosen; the pipeline must make replacement trivial.
implications: Every sprite is addressed by a deterministic key (kind_WxH_theme). A real PNG at the conventional path always wins over the procedural fallback.
No sprite dimensions or offsets are hardcoded in game logic; everything derives from grid size.

designPillars.statement: Any capable model or developer should be able to extend the game by reading docs/ and src/content/.
implications: Docs describe systems by contract (inputs/outputs/invariants), not implementation trivia.
Work is chunked in docs/PLAN.md with explicit status so sessions can resume mid-stream.

coordinateSystem.description: The world is a fixed grid of square cells, side-view cutaway. x grows rightward, y grows downward. Rows [0, groundRow) are sky/surface; rows [groundRow, rows) are underground and buildable.

grid.buildableRegion: x in [0, cols), y in [groundRow, rows)

coordinateSystem.artCellPxNote: Each grid cell is authored at 64x64 art pixels. Rendering scales by camera zoom with image smoothing disabled for crisp pixels. Final art must be authored at exactly moduleWidth*64 x moduleHeight*64. That budget supports high-fidelity '32-bit era' art (full color, no palette limits); if final art needs even more detail, bumping ART_CELL is a contained change (grid.ts constant + retuned procedural painters), since no ids or save data encode pixel sizes.

camera.defaultZoom: 1.5 — one grid cell renders at ~96 CSS px ≈ 1 inch. Modules should feel big and chunky by default; players zoom out for overview.
camera.controls: One-finger / left-drag on empty space pans. Wheel or pinch zooms toward pointer. Zoom clamped to [0.25, 3.0].
camera.persistence: Camera state is session-only, not saved.

moduleCatalog.contract: A list of module KINDS (bedroom, kitchen, ...). Each kind declares: id, display name, category, tags, rarity, and a list of sizes {w,h}. The engine expands kinds x sizes into concrete ModuleDefs with id `${kindId}_${w}x${h}`.

sizeRules.heights: Most modules are 1 tile tall. 2-tall and 3-tall modules exist but are rarer (rarity uncommon/rare).
sizeRules.widths: Most kinds ship multiple width variants, e.g. bedroom in 2, 3, and 4 wide.

moduleCatalog.extensibility: To add a kind: add an entry to modules.json, add a furniture painter in src/render/procedural/furniture.ts (or ship real PNGs), run npm run validate:content.

themes.contract: Exactly the style axis. Every module def gets one sprite per theme. v1 themes: tech, fantasy, realistic, cozy, glam. Each theme declares a pixel-art palette (wall, wallShadow, floor, accent, glow, trim) consumed by the procedural sprite generator.
themes.selection: Player picks a theme per placement (a theme selector in the sidebar filters/styles the catalog). Mixed-theme bases are allowed and encouraged.

environments.contract: A single choice made when starting a base (changeable later without losing placements). One environment bundles three things: aboveground structure, weather, and dirt/soil style. Rendered by src/render/environment.ts.
environments.ambientLife: Weather is ANIMATED in v1 (falling rain, drifting snow and clouds, twinkling stars) via a per-frame overlay on top of a cached static environment canvas. Placed modules land with a 200ms pop. The dirt hides deterministic easter eggs (gem clusters, old bones, worm buddies) for kids to find while scrolling.

rules: A module occupies a w x h rectangle of cells entirely inside the buildable region.
Placements can be moved (drag) and deleted (select then delete / trash button).
placement.interaction: Drag a catalog card onto the canvas (custom pointer-event drag, works with touch). A ghost preview shows validity (green tint valid / red tint invalid) snapped to the grid. Drop places it. Dragging an already-placed module moves it.
placement.undo: Single linear undo/redo stack over placement operations (place, move, delete, clear).

visualConnection.rule: When two placements share an edge segment, the renderer draws a doorway/opening connector across that seam instead of a solid wall, so rooms read as one connected base.
visualConnection.implementation: Sprites are authored/generated with walls; the renderer overlays connector openings at shared edges at draw time. Connection detection = axis-aligned edge overlap of at least 1 cell.
visualConnection.style: Deliberately QUIET: slim arched doorways rising from the floor line, small pass-through windows on upper rows, thin ladderways through floors. Low contrast, whisper-thin edges — connected, not decorated. No chunky frames.

selectionInspector.rule: Tapping a placed module selects it (dashed outline) and opens an inspector panel (bottom-left): kind name + blurb, style dots to restyle THIS placement, size chips to swap to another size of the same kind in place (disabled when the footprint doesn't fit), and Remove. Moving stays direct — drag the module itself.

labelOverlay.rule: A global toggle draws each placement's display name (and size) centered on the module in a readable badge. Off by default.

persistence.autosave: Full base state serialized to localStorage on every mutation (debounced).

saveFormat.shape: { version, environmentId, placements: [{ id, defId, theme, x, y }] }
saveFormat.compatibility: Loaders must check version and migrate or reject gracefully. defIds and themeIds are stable strings — never rename published ids; deprecate instead.

persistence.exportImport: Export downloads the same JSON as a .json file; import loads one. Unknown defIds on import are skipped with a friendly count shown to the player.

artPipeline.spriteKey: `${kindId}_${w}x${h}_${themeId}` — deterministic, derived, never stored in save files.
resolution: 1. If public/art/modules/<spriteKey>.png is listed in public/art/art-manifest.json, load and use it.
2. Otherwise generate a procedural placeholder sprite at runtime (deterministic per key) and cache it.
artPipeline.replacingArt: Drop final PNG(s) into public/art/modules/ at exactly w*64 x h*64 px, add filenames to art-manifest.json (or run the future scan script), reload. Zero code changes.
artPipeline.proceduralStyle: Placeholders are real pixel art, not gray boxes: theme palette walls/floors plus per-kind furniture silhouettes (bed, stove, arcade cabinet...) so the game reads correctly before final art exists.

uiLayout.canvas: Fills the viewport behind everything; owns pan/zoom/placement input.

sidebar.contents: ONE card per module KIND (not per size — keeps the list short). Each card: live thumbnail from the sprite pipeline, name, rarity/blurb, and size chips (2×1 | 3×1 | 4×1...) that pick the variant to drag. The global theme selector restyles all thumbnails and sets the style of new placements.

outOfScopeV1: Animated module interiors (hook: sprite pipeline returns static frames now; a frames[] extension is reserved in the sprite cache API)
Characters walking around the base (hook: placements expose a walkability graph via shared-edge connections — the same data as visualConnection)

roadmap.note: Authoritative live status lives in docs/PLAN.md; this is the design-level phase intent.

phases.name: Foundation
phases.goal: Playable sandbox: pick environment, drag/drop themed modules, pan/zoom, labels, save/export.

phases.name: Catalog explosion
phases.goal: Grow to 30+ kinds, decorations/props layer, more environments and themes.

phases.name: Final art
phases.goal: Replace procedural placeholders with authored pixel art via the drop-in pipeline; per-module animation frames.

phases.name: Life
phases.goal: Characters that walk between connected rooms; ambient module animations; day/night.
